name: Franklin First
description_short: Franklin First Federal Credit Union is a member-owned cooperative bank serving Franklin County, Massachusetts since 1958, offering checking, savings, loans, and credit cards.
description_long:
Franklin First Federal Credit Union traces its origins to 1958, when four separate credit unions—Franklin County Public Hospital FCU, Franklin County Teachers FCU, Lunt Silversmiths, and Greenfield Tap & Die Credit Union—merged to better serve their community. The organization built its central office and branch at 57 Newton Street in Greenfield, Massachusetts in 1995 and obtained community charter status from the National Credit Union Administration in 2000, expanding membership eligibility to anyone living, working, worshiping, or attending school in Franklin County. Today, Franklin First serves over 7,000 members across the region.

Franklin First offers a comprehensive suite of consumer banking products including checking accounts, savings accounts, rewards checking, certificates of deposit, auto loans, personal loans, solar loans, and home loans. They provide online banking access, mobile applications for iOS and Android, and a VISA credit card with dedicated mobile app. Current rate offerings include auto loans from 5.74% APR, personal loans from 13.00% APR, rewards checking up to 4.00% APY, and certificates of deposit up to 3.20% APY as of April 2026.

As a not-for-profit financial cooperative, Franklin First differentiates itself by being member-owned and member-operated, meaning account holders become cooperative owners rather than customers of a commercial bank. The organization emphasizes personalized service and community focus, having served Franklin County for over 65 years. Their merger history demonstrates a commitment to consolidation and growth while maintaining local presence and community connection.

Franklin First is a legitimate, federally-chartered credit union with established operations and a long track record. The main limitation is geographic membership restriction to Franklin County, Massachusetts, which excludes potential customers outside this region. They appear to be a straightforward, traditional credit union without the specialized niche services (debt relief, credit repair, specialized lending) offered by some competitors. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Rewards checking with up to 4.00% APY
pros: Member-owned cooperative structure means profits can benefit members rather than external shareholders
Long operational history dating to 1958 demonstrates stability and community commitment
Competitive auto loan rates starting at 5.74% APR
Rewards checking account with rates up to 4.00% APY
Multiple loan product options including solar loans and home loans
Digital banking options including mobile apps for iOS and Android
Membership remains valid even after relocating, as long as minimum share balance is maintained
Community charter allows membership to anyone in Franklin County, not just employees of a specific employer
cons: Membership strictly limited to Franklin County, Massachusetts—no options for out-of-area applicants
Personal loan rates starting at 13.00% APR are notably higher than auto loan rates, limiting flexibility for non-auto borrowing
Limited online information about account fees, minimum balances, or membership requirements beyond geographic eligibility
No mention of specialized services like credit building, credit monitoring, or financial counseling
Smaller institution with 7,000 members may have fewer resources and ATM network compared to larger banks
best_for: Franklin County, Massachusetts residents seeking a community-focused credit union with personalized service
Borrowers in the region needing auto loans or home financing with competitive rates
Individuals who prefer member-owned financial institutions over commercial banks
Long-term community members who value local relationship banking and reinvestment of profits
diagnosis: Franklin First is best for Franklin County, Massachusetts residents seeking traditional banking services from a stable, member-owned credit union. The primary caveat is strict geographic membership limitation—applicants must live, work, worship, or attend school in Franklin County, making this institution unavailable to consumers outside this region.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
